evals.prompt: Audit the architecture of this NestJS backend — I'm concerned about logic creeping into controllers.
evals.expected_output: Run grep for repository/DB calls in controllers directory. Flag any controller method with >5 lines of business logic. Report layer violations with scoring impact (-15 per instance). Recommend moving logic to Service layer.

evals.prompt: Our React codebase has grown over 2 years. Check if we have a monolith problem — some files might be huge.
evals.expected_output: Search for component files >500 lines (Medium debt) and >1000 lines (Critical). Count hook usage in components/ vs hooks/ folders. Flag files with multiple responsibilities. Recommend splitting by Single Responsibility Principle.

evals.prompt: I see we have both UserService.ts and UserServiceNew.ts in the codebase. Is this a problem?
evals.expected_output: Yes — this is structural duplication (split source of truth). Score: -10 for duplicated legacy entity. Recommend consolidating into one canonical service, migrating callers, and deleting the legacy version. Check for /v1, /v2, or Refactor folders as related patterns.

should_not_trigger: Write a unit test for the user login function
How do I center a div in CSS?
